texto: I'm going to draw up a human-rights contract that says everyone on earth must agree we are here as caretakers of the planet, first and foremost.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Jenni Fagan sobre temas ligados a saude mental. A afirmação “I'm going to draw up a human-rights contract that says everyone on earth must agree we are here a…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a saude-mental, environmentalism, government, útil em momentos de government.

frase_original: I'm going to draw up a human-rights contract that says everyone on earth must agree we are here as caretakers of the planet, first and foremost.

texto: I dinnae get people, like they all want to be watched, to be seen, like all the time. They put up their pictures online and let people they dinnae like look at them! And people they’ve never met as well, and they all pretend tae be shinier than they are – and some are even posting on like four sites; their bosses are watching them at work, the cameras watch them on the bus, and on the train, and in Boots, and even outside the chip shop. Then even at home – they’re going online to look and see who they can watch, and to check who’s watching them!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jenni Fagan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I dinnae get people, like they all want to be watched, to be seen, like all the time. They put up…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, facebook, humorous pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e termino.
